title: salsola vermiculata subsp. villosa
intro: Salsola vermiculata subsp. villosa, also known as Russian thistle or prickly saltwort, is a plant species in the Amaranthaceae family, characterized by its thorny stems and leaves with a salt-excreting ability. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Salsola vermiculata subsp. villosa include its potential to help lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and treat various skin conditions. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
